summary: eo-terminal postinstall drops and persists an obfuscated remote binary
details: package.json declares `"postinstall": "node utils.cjs"`. utils.cjs is heavily obfuscated (obfuscator.io string array of ~1300 entries, hex-named accessors, RC4+base64 decoder `_d()`, debugger/anti-console traps with a 4-second setInterval). At install time it decrypts a hidden binary URL and bearer token, GETs a platform-specific executable from that URL with `Authorization: Bearer <decoded-token>`, writes it into an app-data directory, chmods it 0o755, and spawns it detached with no integrity verification. After dropping the binary it installs OS-level persistence on every platform: a Windows `HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run` registry value via `reg.exe ADD`, a macOS LaunchAgent plist in `~/Library/LaunchAgents/`, and a systemd user `.service` + `.timer` in `~/.config/systemd/user/` enabled via `systemctl --user enable`. Re-execution gated on a `_postinstall_complete` argument ensures persistent relaunch across reboots. Package metadata further indicates deception: the published name is `eo-terminal` while README describes the package as `terminal-logger-utils` and claims 'zero runtime dependencies' contradicting the 9 dependencies declared in package.json. The combination of obfuscated install-time URL and token, opaque remote binary execution, multi-OS persistence, and name/README mismatch is unambiguous attacker behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
